task: I’ve got a messy client note that needs a reply today, and I want to make sure the response is clear enough to send without a lot of back-and-forth. Can you help me shape a few ways to answer it so I can pick the one that fits best?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — send me the client note and I’ll shape a few reply options for you.

If you want, I can give you:
- one very short
- one a little more polished
- one firmer / more decisive

All client-safe, and tight enough to send today.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah — here’s the note, rough as hell:

“Hey, circling back on the concepts. We like the direction overall, but the board felt the options were a little too safe and not distinct enough from what we’ve seen before. Could you tighten the thinking, push the premium feel, and maybe give us something with more edge? Also, a few of the mockups felt rushed — especially around the logo placement and copy hierarchy. We need something we can feel confident presenting internally today if possible.”

I need 3 reply options: short, polished, and firmer. Not overly apologetic. Keep it client-safe but don’t make us sound weak.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are 3 client-safe reply options with different levels of polish:

### 1) Short
Thanks — understood. We’ll tighten the thinking, push the premium feel, and sharpen the hierarchy, especially around logo placement and copy. I’ll send back a more distinct pass that feels stronger for internal review today.

### 2) Polished
Thanks for the clear feedback — that’s helpful. We’ll sharpen the concepts so they feel more distinct, more premium, and less expected, while also tightening the mockup details, especially logo placement and copy hierarchy. We’ll aim to get you a stronger pass you can feel confident sharing internally today.

### 3) Firmer
Understood. We’ll refine the direction to make it feel more distinct, more premium, and less safe, and we’ll clean up the execution points called out — particularly logo placement and copy hierarchy. We’ll send a stronger, more confident pass for internal review today.

If you want, I can also give you:
- one that sounds a little more senior / controlled
- one that’s warmer
- one that pushes back lightly while staying polite

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s solid. Clean enough, not overcooked. The firmer one is probably the one I’d send.

The only thing I’d maybe tweak is making it sound a little more controlled and less like we’re scrambling to fix mistakes. But overall, it lands.
